body.claim: Some element lies in ≥ 0.38 of the sets of any finite union-closed family — an information-theoretic bound short of the conjectured 1/2.
refs: sharpened to (3−√5)/2 ≈ 0.38 by Sawin; Chase–Lovett; Alweiss–Huang–Sellke (2022)

body.claim: Signs achieving ‖Σ εᵢvᵢ‖_∞ = O(√(log n)) always exist — the best known bound, short of the conjectured O(1) constant K.
refs: Banaszczyk 1998 — an O(√log n) discrepancy bound via a convex-geometry argument
